subject: Project Management
title: What the Manifesto Left Out
description: A short lesson to help you apply What the Manifesto Left Out.

hook.text: The Agile Manifesto says nothing about sprints, standups, or story points.

buildup.text: It doesn't mention specific roles, tools, or meeting formats. It's deliberately vague.

discovery.text: The authors believed that prescribing practices would repeat waterfall's mistake.

twist.text: That vagueness let anyone claim to be 'Agile' — including organizations that changed nothing.

climax.text: The manifesto's greatest strength — openness — became its greatest vulnerability.

punchline.text: They left it open. Everyone filled in their own version.

quiz.question: Why did the Agile Manifesto avoid prescribing specific practices?
options: The authors believed prescriptions would repeat waterfall's mistake
They couldn't agree on any practices
They planned to add them later
